name: Jack Kerouac
abstract: Jean-Louis Lebris de Kérouac (; March 12, 1922 – October 21, 1969), known as Jack Kerouac, was an American novelist and poet who, alongside William S. Burroughs and Allen Ginsberg, was a pioneer of the Beat Generation.

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: Kerouac was interested in jazz, and he knew who I was, although we had never met. Eventually... In that year, we did meet, because my brother Al... would like to interview you... One weekend I sat down and talked to him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Dave Solomon... Metronome during his tenure published William Burroughs... He published a poem by Jack Kerouac.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
